attribute.name: Test Execution & Coverage
attribute.description: Single-command test runner with adequate coverage configuration
attribute.criteria: Runnable tests with coverage config

evidence: Go test command found in project files

attribute.name: Type Annotations
attribute.description: Type hints in function signatures
attribute.criteria: >80% of functions have type annotations

evidence: Go enforces types at compile time (statically typed)

attribute.name: CLAUDE.md Configuration Files
attribute.description: Project-specific configuration for AI coding agents
attribute.criteria: CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md file exists in repository root

evidence: AGENTS.md is the cross-tool standard supported by Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Codex, and Gemini CLI

attribute.name: CI Quality Gates
attribute.description: CI runs lint, type-check, and tests on every PR
attribute.criteria: CI gates with lint + type-check + tests

findings.threshold: CI with lint + test + type-check gates on PRs

attribute.name: Single-File Verification
attribute.description: Single-file lint and type-check commands available for fast feedback
attribute.criteria: Documented single-file lint/type-check commands

evidence: No single-file verification commands found in context files

remediation.summary: Document single-file lint and type-check commands in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
steps: Add single-file lint command to context file (e.g., 'ruff check path/to/file.py')
Add single-file type-check command (e.g., 'mypy path/to/file.py')
Ensure these commands work without a full build step
Target <5 seconds execution per file

attribute.name: README Structure
attribute.description: Well-structured README with key sections
attribute.criteria: README.md with installation, usage, and development sections

attribute.name: Standard Project Layouts
attribute.description: Follows standard project structure for language
attribute.criteria: Standard directories (src/ or project-named, tests/) present

attribute.name: Dependency Pinning for Reproducibility
attribute.description: Dependencies pinned to exact versions in lock files
attribute.criteria: Lock file with pinned versions, updated within 6 months

findings.threshold: lock file with pinned versions, < 6 months old

attribute.name: Dependency Security & Vulnerability Scanning
attribute.description: Security scanning tools configured for dependencies and code
attribute.criteria: Dependabot, Renovate, CodeQL, or SAST tools configured; secret detection enabled

remediation.summary: Add more security scanning tools for comprehensive coverage
steps: Enable Dependabot alerts in GitHub repository settings (or configure Renovate: add renovate.json to repository root)
Add CodeQL scanning workflow for SAST
Configure secret detection (detect-secrets, gitleaks)
Set up language-specific scanners (pip-audit, npm audit, Snyk)
commands: pip install detect-secrets  # Python secret detection
examples:
# .github/dependabot.yml
version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: pip
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: weekly

attribute.name: Conventional Commit Messages
attribute.description: Follows conventional commit format
attribute.criteria: ≥80% of recent commits follow convention

evidence: No commitlint configuration found (.commitlintrc.json, package.json, husky, or pre-commit)

steps: Option A (Python/pre-commit): Add conventional-pre-commit to .pre-commit-config.yaml
Option B (JS/commitlint): Install commitlint and configure husky for commit-msg hook
commands: pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install --hook-type commit-msg

steps: Review GitHub's gitignore templates for your language
Add the 7 missing patterns
Ensure editor/IDE patterns are included

attribute.name: One-Command Build/Setup
attribute.description: Single command to set up development environment from fresh clone
attribute.criteria: Single command (make setup, npm install, etc.) documented prominently

evidence: Setup command found in README: 'make build'

attribute.name: File Size Limits
attribute.description: Files are reasonably sized for AI context windows
attribute.criteria: <5% of files >500 lines, no files >1000 lines

attribute.name: Separation of Concerns
attribute.description: Code organized with single responsibility per module
attribute.criteria: Feature-based organization, cohesive modules, low coupling

attribute.name: Concise Documentation
attribute.description: Documentation maximizes information density while minimizing token consumption
attribute.criteria: README <500 lines with clear structure, bullet points over prose

evidence: Only 8 bullet points (prefer bullets over prose)

remediation.summary: Make documentation more concise and structured
steps: Break long README into multiple documents (docs/ directory)
Add clear Markdown headings (##, ###) for structure
Convert prose paragraphs to bullet points where possible
Add table of contents for documents >100 lines
Use code blocks instead of describing commands in prose
Move detailed content to wiki or docs/, keep README focused
examples:
# Good: Concise with structure

## Quick Start
```bash
pip install -e .
agentready assess .
```

## Features
- Fast repository scanning
- HTML and Markdown reports
- 25 agent-ready attributes

## Documentation
See [docs/](docs/) for detailed guides.
# Bad: Verbose prose

This project is a tool that helps you assess your repository
against best practices for AI-assisted development. It works by
scanning your codebase and checking for various attributes that
make repositories more effective when working with AI coding
assistants like Claude Code...

[Many more paragraphs of prose...]

citations.title: LongCodeBench: Evaluating Coding LLMs at 1M Context Windows
citations.relevance: Research showing performance degradation with long contexts

attribute.name: Inline Documentation
attribute.description: Function, class, and module-level documentation using language-specific conventions
attribute.criteria: ≥80% of public functions/classes have docstrings

remediation.summary: Add godoc comments to exported Go symbols
steps: Add comments above all exported functions, types, and constants
Comments must start with the symbol name (Go convention)
Add doc.go files for package-level documentation
Add Example functions in test files for executable docs

attribute.name: Pattern References for Common Changes
attribute.description: Reference implementations and skills for common change types
attribute.criteria: 3-5 pattern references or skills documented

evidence: No pattern references or skills found

remediation.summary: Add pattern references for 3-5 common change types
steps: Identify your 3-5 most common change types (new endpoint, new component, etc.)
For each, point to one real example in the codebase
Create .claude/skills/ directory with SKILL.md files for detailed patterns
Or add a 'Pattern References' section to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
examples:
# In CLAUDE.md:
## Pattern References
- New API endpoint: follow the pattern in `src/api/handlers/users.ts`
- New adapter: see `src/adapters/postgres.py` as reference

citations.title: Repository Scaffolding for AI Coding Agents, Section 2.1
citations.relevance: Pattern references turn novel changes into copy-modify changes

citations.title: Claude Code Skills Documentation
citations.relevance: Skills system for on-demand pattern knowledge

attribute.name: Design Intent Documentation
attribute.description: Documented preconditions, invariants, and design rationale
attribute.criteria: Design docs with architectural intent

remediation.summary: Document design intent: preconditions, invariants, and rationale
steps: Create docs/design/ directory
For each critical module, document preconditions, invariants, and rationale
Use an AI agent to reverse-engineer initial design docs from code, then enrich with intent
Reference design docs from CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
examples:
# docs/design/event-system.md
## Invariants
- Event log is append-only; never mutate or delete entries
- Events are processed exactly-once via idempotency keys

## Preconditions
- Auth middleware must validate token before event handlers run

## Rationale
- Polling instead of webhooks: upstream API has 5s delivery SLA, too slow for our use case

citations.title: Repository Scaffolding for AI Coding Agents, Section 2.3
citations.relevance: Agents cannot infer design intent from code alone

attribute.name: Repomix AI Context Generation
attribute.description: Automated repository context generation for AI consumption
attribute.criteria: Repomix configured with fresh output (< 7 days old)

remediation.summary: Configure Repomix for AI-friendly context generation
steps: Initialize Repomix: agentready repomix-generate --init
Generate context: agentready repomix-generate
Add to bootstrap: agentready bootstrap --repomix
Set up GitHub Action for automatic updates
examples:
# Initialize Repomix configuration
agentready repomix-generate --init

# Generate repository context
agentready repomix-generate

# Check freshness
agentready repomix-generate --check

attribute.name: Cyclomatic Complexity Thresholds

attribute.criteria: Average complexity <10, no functions >15

steps: Install with: go install github.com/fzipp/gocyclo/cmd/gocyclo@latest

attribute.name: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
attribute.description: Lightweight documents capturing architectural decisions
attribute.criteria: ADR directory with documented decisions

evidence: No ADR directory found (checked docs/adr/, docs/architecture/, docs/design/, docs/specs/, specs/, and variants — all case-insensitive)

remediation.summary: Create Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) directory and document key decisions
steps: Create docs/adr/ directory in repository root
Use Michael Nygard ADR template or MADR format
Document each significant architectural decision
Number ADRs sequentially (0001-*.md, 0002-*.md)
Include Status, Context, Decision, and Consequences sections
Update ADR status when decisions are revised (Superseded, Deprecated)
commands: We need to record architectural decisions made in this project.
We will use Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) as described by Michael Nygard.
examples:
# Example ADR Structure

```markdown
# 2. Use PostgreSQL for Database

Date: 2025-11-22

## Status
Accepted

## Context
We need a relational database for complex queries and ACID transactions.
Team has PostgreSQL experience. Need full-text search capabilities.

## Decision
Use PostgreSQL 15+ as primary database.

## Consequences
- Positive: Robust ACID, full-text search, team familiarity
- Negative: Higher resource usage than SQLite
- Neutral: Need to manage migrations, backups
```

attribute.name: Structured Logging
attribute.description: Logging in structured format (JSON) with consistent fields
attribute.criteria: Structured logging library configured (structlog, winston, zap)

evidence: Structured logging library found: zap, logrus

attribute.name: OpenAPI/Swagger Specifications
attribute.description: Machine-readable API documentation in OpenAPI format
attribute.criteria: OpenAPI 3.x spec with complete endpoint documentation

evidence: Searched recursively for: openapi.yaml, openapi.yml, openapi.json, swagger.yaml, swagger.yml, swagger.json

remediation.summary: Create OpenAPI specification for API endpoints
steps: Create openapi.yaml in repository root
Define OpenAPI version 3.x
Document all API endpoints with full schemas
Add request/response examples
Define security schemes (API keys, OAuth, etc.)
Validate spec with Swagger Editor or Spectral
Generate API documentation with Swagger UI or ReDoc
examples:
# openapi.yaml - Minimal example
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: My API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: API for managing users

servers:
  - url: https://api.example.com/v1

paths:
  /users/{userId}:
    get:
      summary: Get user by ID
      parameters:
        - name: userId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: User found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/User'
        '404':
          description: User not found

components:
  schemas:
    User:
      type: object
      required:
        - id
        - email
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          example: "user_123"
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
          example: "user@example.com"
        name:
          type: string
          example: "John Doe"

attribute.name: Issue & Pull Request Templates
attribute.description: Standardized templates for issues and PRs
attribute.criteria: PR template and issue templates in .github/

remediation.summary: Create GitHub issue and PR templates in .github/ directory
steps: Create .github/ directory if it doesn't exist
Add PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md for PRs
Create .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ directory
Add bug_report.md for bug reports
Add feature_request.md for feature requests
Optionally add config.yml to configure template chooser
Note: org-level templates in <org>/.github are also recognized
commands: <!-- Describe the changes in this PR -->
examples:
# Bug Report Template (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md)

```markdown
---
name: Bug Report
about: Create a report to help us improve
title: '[BUG] '
labels: bug
assignees: ''
---

**Describe the bug**
A clear description of what the bug is.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce:
1. Go to '...'
2. Click on '....'
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
What you expected to happen.

**Environment**
- OS: [e.g. macOS 13.0]
- Version: [e.g. 1.0.0]
```

citations.title: About issue and pull request templates
